text: The perimeter of ∆ABC is 36 cm and its in radius is 8 cm. Then, the area of the triangle is
name: Questions Item 1

text: Let a, b and c be the lengths of the sides of a triangle with its opposite angles A, B, and C respectively. If ∠C = 60°, then the value of \dfrac{(c+a-b)(a+b-c)}{(b+c-a)(c+a-b)} is
explanation: $$\cos 60^\circ = \dfrac{a^2 + b^2 - c^2}{2ab} \implies ab = a^2 + b^2 - c^2.$$ The expression simplifies to 1.
name: Questions Item 2

text: In a △ABC, ∠C=60° and ∠A=75°. If D is a point on AC such that the area of △BAD is 3 times the area of △BCD, then the measure of ∠ABD is
explanation: $$\dfrac{AD}{CD}=3 \implies \angle ABD=30^\circ$$ using sine rule in △ABD and △BCD.
